title: semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora
intro: Semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora, commonly known as the Australian Fan Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of the Australian Fan Flower are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Research suggests that the bioactive constituents in the Australian Fan Flower may help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress.
side_effects: Semele androgyna subsp. pterygophora can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Ingestion of large quantities can lead to more severe complications, including liver damage and kidney failure. Improper use of the plant can also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. If not handled carefully, it can also cause eye irritation and vision problems.
